hero.title: Building the Web3 Virtual Goods Ecosystem
hero.subtitle: Sapphire Mall is committed to building the world's leading Web3 virtual goods trading platform, providing users with secure, efficient, and rewarding experiences through contribution incentives, DAO governance, and diversified asset support.

coreValues.title: Core Values
coreValues.subtitle: We are committed to redefining virtual goods trading and creating real value and meaning for the Web3 ecosystem

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.content: Build a decentralized, secure, and user-friendly Web3 virtual goods trading platform. Through contribution incentives and DAO governance, enable every participant to share in the platform's growth, truly achieving value co-creation and benefit sharing.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.content: Become the world's leading Web3 virtual goods trading ecosystem, connecting millions of users and creators, promoting the prosperity of the virtual economy, enabling digital assets to play greater value in the real world, and building a new era of digital economy that truly belongs to the community.

revenueSharing.title: 70% Revenue Sharing
revenueSharing.desc: World's first 70% platform fees returned to active contributors

security.title: Multi-layer Security
security.desc: Smart contract audits, KYC verification, fund escrow

governance.title: Community Governance
governance.desc: DAO governance, community decisions, transparent and open

ecosystem.title: Global Ecosystem
ecosystem.desc: Multi-chain support, multi-language service, global coverage

opportunity.title: Market Opportunity
items: • Rapid growth of Web3 gaming users
• Surging demand for digital asset trading
• Traditional platforms' high fee pain points

multiAsset.title: Massive Commodity Trading
multiAsset.desc: Support for gaming items, digital art, virtual land, music, fashion, and collectibles across multiple platforms.

analytics.title: Advanced Analytics

team.title: Team Introduction
team.content: Sapphire Mall is built by an experienced Web3 team, with members from renowned technology companies and blockchain projects, possessing rich experience in decentralized finance, smart contract development, product design, and global operations. We are committed to solving the pain points of traditional virtual goods trading through technological innovation.

experience.title: Industry Experience

innovation.title: Innovation Driven
innovation.desc: Continuously exploring Web3 technology frontiers to provide users with the best trading experience

howItWorks.title: How Sapphire Mall works
howItWorks.subtitle: From browsing to on-chain settlement in three clear steps.

steps.title: Browse & select
steps.desc: Explore virtual goods in the marketplace, pick a SKU, and review pricing before checkout.

steps.title: Pay with your wallet
steps.desc: Connect a Web3 wallet and pay with USDC, SAP, or other supported tokens on your chosen network.

steps.title: Confirm on-chain
steps.desc: PaymentRouter records the order on-chain; the platform updates order status after confirmation.

howItWorks.previewCaption: Click a step to preview the DApp screen — launch the app for the full experience.

previewSteps.browse: Sapphire Mall marketplace — browse and select products
previewSteps.pay: Wallet payment — authorize and pay with USDC or SAP

roadmap.title: Product roadmap
roadmap.subtitle: Where we are headed — milestones may adjust as the ecosystem evolves.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers before you connect your wallet.

items.a: Any wallet compatible with WalletConnect / injected providers supported by our DApp (e.g. MetaMask).

items.q: Which tokens can I pay with?
items.a: USDC and SAP are supported on payment chains; Arc Testnet also supports EURC and cirBTC where configured.
SAP is listed in the payment UI; on-chain SAP checkout is rolling out — USDC is recommended for production tests.

items.q: How does contributor revenue sharing work?
items.a: Active contributors earn a share of platform fees according to published tokenomics — see the whitepaper for details.
Use the Help center, documentation links below, or contact us in the Support section.

newsletter.success: Thanks — we will keep you posted.
newsletter.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address.

legal.privacyBody: We collect only what is needed to operate the marketplace and improve the product. Wallet addresses used for transactions are recorded on-chain; contact details you provide are used for order support. We do not sell personal data. For questions, contact support@sapphiremall.com.
legal.termsBody: By using Sapphire Mall you agree to transact at your own risk on public blockchains. Virtual goods are subject to seller terms and applicable laws. The platform provides infrastructure only and does not custody user funds beyond smart-contract flows described in our documentation.
legal.cookieBody: We use essential cookies and local storage to remember language preferences and improve site performance. We do not use third-party advertising cookies. You can clear cookies via your browser settings at any time.

cover.title: Sapphire Mall Introduction
cover.subtitle: A community-driven decentralized marketplace for virtual goods

intro.title: Platform Introduction
intro.intro: Sapphire Mall is a blockchain-based decentralized marketplace for virtual goods, centered on DAO governance with SAP (ERC-20) as the ecosystem token. Payment strengths:

bullets.title: Stablecoin payments
bullets.desc: Arc Testnet integration for USDC, EURC, cirBTC and more. Goods priced in USDC with near-instant on-chain settlement and low gas.

bullets.title: Bridge-free cross-chain
bullets.desc: Pay with assets from multiple chains without manual bridging. PaymentRouter and SettlementVault unify fund routing.

bullets.title: SAP incentives
bullets.desc: SAP as a native payment option with fee discounts and community incentives — a trade–reward–govern loop.

overview.title: Platform Core Overview
overview.subtitle: Core building blocks and operating logic of Sapphire Mall

rows.title: Blockchain foundation
rows.desc: Built on blockchain with smart contracts for core logic — secure, transparent, and immutable.

rows.title: Stablecoins + SAP
rows.desc: Arc-based stablecoin payments plus SAP for second-level settlement and cross-chain exchange.

rows.title: Community co-governance
rows.desc: DAO governance lets buyers, creators, and contributors decide and share upside.

rows.title: Multilingual & global
rows.desc: Built-in bilingual support and responsive UX for a global audience.

architecture.title: Technical Architecture
architecture.subtitle: How the stack and payment system work

rows.title: Dual-track payments
rows.desc: Stablecoins for stability and SAP for governance incentives.

rows.title: Rules on contracts
rows.desc: Core rules live on-chain; order and asset state are publicly verifiable.

rows.title: Traceable orders
rows.desc: Full order lifecycle is recorded on-chain for auditability and fairness.

rows.title: Multi-chain ready
rows.desc: Roadmap includes Ethereum, Polygon, BSC and more to expand users and assets.

painpoints.title: Traditional E-commerce Pain Points
bullets: Opaque listing fees, deposits, and marketing spend — hard for merchants to audit.
Funds sit in centralized escrow with counterparty and misappropriation risk.
Disputes follow unilateral platform rules that users cannot verify.
Unclear rights and revenue sharing for virtual goods hurt creators.
High cross-border costs and weak governance — users miss platform upside.
Long, opaque settlement cycles between platforms and merchants.

web2limits.title: Web2 E-commerce Limits
web2limits.subtitle: Typical problems and trust crises in traditional commerce

cells.title: Opaque fees
cells.desc: Listing fees, deposits, and marketing budgets are hard to audit; platforms hold absolute power.

cells.title: Custody risk
cells.desc: Centralized escrow and clearing create counterparty and misappropriation risk.

cells.title: Unfair disputes
cells.desc: Outcomes rely on unilateral rules; users cannot verify fairness.

cells.title: Costly cross-border
cells.desc: Heavy regulation and fees, with limited paths for user governance.

merchant.title: Merchant Limits & Impact
merchant.subtitle: Pain points transfer downstream and hurt merchant vitality

cols.title: Long settlement cycles
cols.desc: Opaque settlement ties up working capital and slows turnover.

cols.title: Weak creator rights
cols.desc: Unclear rights, copyright, and revenue splits undermine creator income.

cols.title: Data monopoly
cols.desc: Platforms own user data; merchants cannot build their own customer assets.

settlement.title: On-chain Settlement & Payments
settlement.subtitle: An efficient, transparent on-chain clearing system

cards.title: Stablecoin options
cards.desc: EURC, USDC and more with cross-chain payments — familiar and stable.

cards.title: On-chain clearing path
cards.desc: Clear settlement paths with real-time status — short cycles, transparent process.

cards.title: Token incentives
cards.desc: SAP powers incentives and governance, binding value to participants.

cards.title: Auditable status
cards.desc: Orders and assets on-chain — anyone can query and audit.

global.title: Globalization & Experience
global.subtitle: A borderless, friendly Web3 user experience

cards.title: Built-in i18n
cards.desc: Chinese/English in the DApp and docs to serve global users.

cards.title: Clear trade feedback
cards.desc: Wallet, network, and balance feedback reduce user mistakes.

cards.title: Responsive Web3 UI
cards.desc: Dark Web3 visuals and responsive layout for desktop and mobile.

community.title: Community & Value Alignment
community.subtitle: Share platform value via DAO governance and multi-role design

cards.title: DAO governance
cards.desc: SAP holders propose and vote on fees, features, and treasury use.

cards.title: Multi-role revenue
cards.desc: Buyers, creators, and contributors share outcomes under one rule set.

cards.title: Transparent treasury
cards.desc: Fees enter the DAO treasury; use is decided by the community.

cards.title: Consensus-driven ops
cards.desc: Decisions shift from a single operator to community consensus.

bullets.title: Users / Buyers
bullets.desc: Browse and buy virtual goods; earn SAP via governance, review, arbitration, and tasks.

bullets.title: Creators / Merchants
bullets.desc: List after certification (IPFS-ready); creators keep sales; platform takes fees by rule.

bullets.title: Contributors / Jurors
bullets.desc: Review listings and disputes for incentives; qualifications link to SAP.

bullets.title: Platform / DAO treasury
bullets.desc: Fees fund buybacks, incentives, or building — decided by the DAO.

bullets.title: Users / Buyers
bullets.desc: Browse and buy; join governance, review, arbitration, and tasks for SAP or rights.

bullets.title: Creators / Merchants
bullets.desc: List after certification; creators keep sales revenue; platform fees by rule.

bullets.title: Contributors / Jurors
bullets.desc: Review and adjudicate for incentives; jury and deposits link to SAP.

bullets.title: Platform / DAO treasury
bullets.desc: Fees enter the treasury for buybacks, incentives, or build-out.

fourRoles.title: Four Major Roles
fourRoles.subtitle: Clear identities and duties for every participant

cards.title: Users / Buyers
cards.desc: Browse and buy virtual goods; join governance, review, and arbitration for SAP.

cards.title: Creators / Merchants
cards.desc: List after certification with IPFS-ready storage; keep full sales revenue.

cards.title: Contributors / Jurors
cards.desc: Review products and disputes to keep the ecosystem healthy — earn incentives.

cards.title: Platform / DAO treasury
cards.desc: Core fund manager receiving fees and allocating per DAO decisions.

roleIncentives.title: Role Earnings & Incentives
roleIncentives.subtitle: Economic returns and motivation for every role

cards.title: Creator sales income
cards.desc: Creators set prices and keep sales; the platform takes a small fee.

cards.title: Juror review rewards
cards.desc: Contributors acting as jurors earn SAP or equity by contribution.

cards.title: Buyer governance rights
cards.desc: SAP holders vote on direction and share ecosystem growth.

cards.title: Treasury allocation
cards.desc: Treasury funds buybacks, incentives, and long-term build tied to growth.
